rationale: The cited paper is used in the Method section to justify the study's two-wave longitudinal survey design. The authors explicitly cite Rindfleisch et al. (2008) to support the claim that longitudinal data are more appropriate for discerning relationships between evaluations/behaviors and later performance outcomes. This is a clear methodological use tied to research design choices, not just a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies guidance from Rindfleisch et al. (2008) to motivate using a two-wave longitudinal survey rather than relying only on cross-sectional data. The citation supports the design logic for temporal separation in testing relationships involving later perceived performance.
